title: Micron shares perk up premarket on news Japan to grant it $320 million subsidy to make advanced memory chips at Hiroshima plant
text: Micron Technology Inc. shares MU, -1.94% perked up in premarket trade Friday, after news that Japan will give the company a subsidy of up to 46.5 billion yen ($320 million) to make advanced memory chips at its Hiroshima plant. The news was announced by Japan's industry ministry, Reuters reported.

title: Japan to give Micron Technology up to $320 mln to boost chip output in Hiroshima -paper
text: Japan will provide U.S. chipmaker Micron Technology with subsidies of up to 46.5 billion yen ($320 million) in investment support so it can build advanced memory chips at its plant in Hirsohima, the Chugoku Shimbun newspaper reported on Friday.

"Just this week, we've seen First Solar FSLR, , Toyota TM, -1.45% , Honda HMC, -1.30% , and Corning GLW, -1.92% make major announcements of new investments and new jobs as a direct result of my economic plan," Biden said in a statement.

title: Micron Technology investing $15 bln in new plant in Boise, Idaho
text: Micron Technology Inc. MU, +0.41% said Thursday it plans to invest about $15 billion through the end of the decade for a new memory manufacturing plant in Boise, Idaho. With anticipated federal grants and credits through the CHIPS and Science Act as well as state incentives, the new fab will create 2,000 direct Micron jobs and more than 17,000 new jobs, the company said.
